name: LG C5 65-inch single-fragment cinematic share
globalControl: One continuous 16-second cinematic vertical phone-shot scene with realistic iPhone 14 native image quality, subtle handheld breathing shake, slight natural motion blur, sincere late-night friend-home-visit conversation mood, modern lived-in living room, no overlays, no recording indicators, no UI, TV always off with deep black reflective mirror screen, no second person visible on camera, only off-camera friend voice.

fragmentList.videoContent: Create a single continuous 16-second vertical cinematic handheld shot with no segment split in the submitted payload. The scene is a sincere, cozy late-night home visit where a woman casually ends up recommending her newly replaced oversized OLED TV to her best friend who remains off camera the entire time. The whole video must feel like a friend casually filming on an iPhone 14 while visiting after work. Keep natural handheld breathing shake, slight vertical bob when stepping forward, soft autofocus drift, slight motion blur, and realistic consumer-phone exposure behavior. Setting: a clean but lived-in modern living room. Required physical details: a minimalist black remote on the TV console, a half-peeled orange on the sofa side table, a half-open balcony sliding door in the background, an unopened tissue box on the edge of the TV console, and a short visible black power cable. Lighting: only warm white recessed ceiling downlights, creating soft highlights on the protagonist's hair, faint natural shadows in the eye sockets, slight uneven wall exposure, and natural shadow noise. Protagonist: East Asian woman around 29 years old, shoulder-length dark brown hair with naturally inward-curled ends and a few wispy bangs, oval face, defined nose bridge, natural untamed brows, glossy muted rose-bean lips, fair skin with visible pores and slight natural oil sheen around the nose. Wardrobe: off-white chunky knit lounge sweater, loose collar showing the collarbone, sleeves casually rolled to the elbows. Product: LG 65 Inch Class C5 Series TV. The TV must always appear extremely large, front-dominant, premium, ultra-thin, and tightly flush to the wall or wall-mounted with virtually zero gap, never moved, never detached from the wall, and always turned off with a deep pure-black mirror-like reflective screen. Composition: the woman occupies one side of the frame while the full large TV is clearly visible behind her from a mostly front-facing angle with slight side reveal. The TV must look huge relative to her body, emphasizing its 65-inch scale. Performance: she looks slightly left of camera toward the off-camera friend rather than into the lens, speaking quickly and naturally with subtle weight shifts, not like an actress. During seconds 0 to 8, she smiles and lightly jokes that her old TV always looked too gray and dull, while the camera starts in a slightly high wide angle showing the living room corner and the full TV. Around seconds 3 to 5, she unconsciously brushes the loose hair near her cheek and lightly bites her lower lip while thinking how to describe the new image quality. During seconds 8 to 16, keep the same scene and continuity. She quickly blinks twice, leans subtly toward the TV, and around seconds 12 to 14 straightens up slightly, turns less than 30 degrees, gently taps the ultra-thin side bezel of the TV with her finger knuckle, then places her palm on the metal frame as if appreciating the premium material, nodding with satisfaction. In the last 2 seconds, the camera pulls back slightly with a tiny laugh-induced wobble to reveal the full imposing TV tight against the wall and her gesture toward the lower-right area of the screen. Dialogue must be spoken naturally in fast English across the full take. First part dialogue: This LG 65-inch TV is an absolute game changer for my movie nights. I can finally see every single stunning detail in perfect 4K resolution right now. Second part dialogue: Plus, OLED evo tech makes colors pop like crazy. WebOS is super easy to use. If you need an upgrade, click the link right now! At the end, the off-camera friend says briefly: So high-end. Negative constraints: absolutely no TV content playback, no system interface, no overlay text, no subtitles, no recording red dot, no other person in frame, no reaching hands toward the camera, no spin over 30 degrees, no studio lighting, no showroom perfection, no visible gap between the TV and wall.
fragmentList.productReferenceDescription: LG 65 Inch Class C5 Series OLED TV, giant 65-inch premium television, ultra-thin metal frame, very large front-facing screen, perfectly wall-flush installation, screen turned off with deep black glossy mirror finish.
fragmentList.positivePrompt: cinematic but realistic social video, lived-in home, vertical handheld phone realism, wall-flush OLED TV, giant realistic 65-inch scale, sincere recommendation, subtle motion blur, premium metal edge detail
fragmentList.negativePrompt: screen UI, overlays, recording icon, subtitles, TV playing content, TV away from wall, visible wall gap, second person visible, studio light, showroom minimalism, overacting, lens-reaching hand, spin over 30 degrees
